problem_71.natural_language: The space of symmetric matrices has dimension `n (n + 1) / 2`.
problem_71.lean:
theorem finrank_symm_matrices
    {K : Type*} [Field K] {n : Nat}
    (S : Submodule K (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K))
    (hS : (S : Set (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K)) = {A | Matrix.IsSymm A}) :
    Module.finrank K S = n * (n + 1) / 2
problem_71.natural_language_proof:
Proof: a symmetric matrix is determined by the entries on and above the diagonal: there are `n`
diagonal entries and `n*(n-1)/2` strictly upper-diagonal entries. Equivalently, consider the
following family of symmetric matrices: the diagonal matrix units `E_{ii}` and, for `i < j`, the
symmetrized off-diagonal matrices `E_{ij} + E_{ji}`. These matrices are linearly independent, and
every symmetric matrix is a unique linear combination of them by reading off its diagonal and
upper-diagonal entries. Hence they form a basis of the symmetric subspace, whose size is
`n + n*(n-1)/2 = n*(n+1)/2`.
